channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: One Nav, Two Stacks: A Microfrontend Between Magento and Laravel Without Replatforming
item.description: A working reference implementation on two production-grade stacks (Magento 2.4 + Laravel 11), with the host integration shape shown below and a server-rendered nav skeleton shipped on day one -...

item.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
item.description: Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the...

item.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
item.description: Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. During an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook...

item.title: Cross-Repo Handoff Protocol: Coordinating AI Coding Agents Across Separate Repositories
item.description: AI coding agents are getting incredibly good at working inside a single repository. They can inspect files, pick up on local conventions, write tests, and churn out solid code. But let's be real:...

item.title: Inside ShopPilot’s AI Content Engine
item.description: Every time we showed a Shopify merchant a generic AI-generated post, they spotted it instantly. Not because AI can't write â€” it can. Because it doesn't know them. The post would be grammatically...

item.title: Beyond the Boring Resume: How I Built an Immersive AI Portfolio with React & Spline
item.description: As a Full Stack & AI/ML Engineer, my daily workflow involves architecting LLM pipelines, scaling backend infrastructure, and dealing with complex data layers. But when it came to presenting my own...

item.title: Patch Your Kernel NOW: 732byte Python rootkit, cracks all distros since 2017
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/rootsecdev/cve_2026_31431 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47968027 Points: 3 # Comments: 1

item.title: Tolerância a Falhas: Como sistemas modernos continuam funcionando mesmo quando tudo dá errado
item.description: Em um mundo cada vez mais dependente de sistemas digitais, a indisponibilidade de serviços pode causar prejuízos financeiros, perda de credibilidade e impacto direto na experiência do usuário....

item.title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel and WHM
item.description: Article URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/hackers-are-actively-exploiting-a-bug-in-cpanel-used-by-millions-of-websites/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47967974 Points: 4 #...

item.title: I audited 25 top npm packages with a zero-install CLI. Here's who passes.
item.description: npx proof-of-commitment react zod chalk lodash axios typescript That's it. No install, no API key, no account. Run it against any package — or drop your package.json at getcommit.dev/audit. I ran...

item.title: You've probably never heard of these npm packages. They're in your production app.
item.description: Everyone knows about chalk (422M weekly downloads, 1 maintainer) and axios (100M/wk, 1 maintainer — compromised March 30, 2026). Those are in your package.json. You chose them. But when we ran the...

item.title: Does ClickHouse Support UPDATEs? A 2026 Data Analysis
item.description: TL;DR Yes, ClickHouse fully supports UPDATEs. As of April 2026, ClickHouse ships standard SQL UPDATE ... SET ... WHERE syntax that runs in milliseconds, alongside four other update mechanisms:...

item.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
item.description: CopyFail threatens multi-tenant servers, CI/CD work flows, Kubernetes containers, and more.

item.title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
item.description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf and now have dueling ad campaigns.

item.title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
item.description: Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. We've already got...

item.title: Rivian downsizes new EV factory after Trump’s DOE slashes loan agreement
item.description: Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000...

item.title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
item.description: Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down...

item.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
item.description: OpenAI will begin rolling out its cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox mode is now available for all Windows 11 PCs
item.description: Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as...

item.title: Meta threatens to pull its apps from New Mexico if forced to make ‘technologically impractical’ changes
item.description: Meta says it may be forced to pull Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp from New Mexico if the attorney general gets his way. The state is demanding a host of changes that the company says are...

item.title: With Saros, Housemarque makes a case for doing next-gen games differently
item.description: It is generally frowned upon to care too much about appearances. We have a lot of little aphorisms discouraging this - books and their covers, beauty being skin deep, style over substance, that...

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we get into how the Elon Musk-Sam Altman trial goes way beyond their rivalry and could have major implications both for OpenAI and also the AI industry at large.

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: The ultimate plan to live forever is a brand new body. This subscriber-only eBook explores R3 Bio, a small startup that has pitched a startling and ethically charged vision for “brainless clones”...

item.title: OpenAI announces new advanced security for ChatGPT accounts, including a partnership with Yubico
item.description: OpenAI is launching additional opt-in protections for ChatGPT accounts. The new security initiative includes a new partnership with security key provider Yubico.

item.title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
item.description: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

item.title: FDA approval, fundraising, and the reality of building in healthcare according to BioticsAI founder
item.description: BioticsAI CEO Robhy Bustami joined Isabelle Johannessen on Build Mode to discuss how the company has navigated a highly regulated space and kept the team motivated while cutting through all the...

item.title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles
item.description: The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience.

item.title: Elon Musk Seemingly Admits xAI Has Used OpenAI’s Models to Train Its Own
item.description: While answering questions under oath, Musk argued it’s standard practice for AI labs to use their competitors’ models.

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out ‘Advanced’ Security Mode for At-Risk Accounts
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out Advanced Account Security for people concerned that their ChatGPT or Codex accounts could be potential targets of phishing attacks.
